meta.setting: timbered hard-rock mine tunnel under the Jefferson hills
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in underground mining danger

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining and extractive labor pressure in the Whiskeytown area.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Mining-era settlement material and gold-rush context.

image_prompt.subject: a timbered hard-rock tunnel with ore car rails, candle stubs, hand drills, powder boxes, and miners moving through damp smoke
image_prompt.scene: dim underground shift moment; one miner braces an ore car, another studies a cracked timber, seep water shines in the ruts, no readable labels
image_prompt.composition: square tunnel-depth image with timber frames receding into darkness and figures caught between candle light and black drift
image_prompt.camera: shoulder-height in-engine exploration view from inside the tunnel, close to rails and wet timber
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the dark practical: the viewer should worry about footing, air, and the next brace.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine mine interior
render_traits: slightly simplified miners, tools, ore car, and tunnel geometry
modest texture resolution on damp wood, rock, iron, mud, cloth, and smoke
hard ambient occlusion under timber caps, rail ties, ore cart, and figures
small warm candle pools against black drift shadow
believable 1899 mining tools and work clothes
claustrophobic playable composition with no polished cinematic gloss
style_modifiers.palette: lamp amber, wet timber brown, rock gray, powder smoke blue, iron black, mud umber, and candle wax cream.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable crate labels, warning signs, chalk marks, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The tunnel, timber sets, ore car, miners, candle light, and seep water are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to 1899 hard-rock mining.
The image supports scenes about danger, labor, pursuit, evidence, and trapped movement.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
